preFix.title: Lemon meringue roulade
preFix.subtitle: Soft meringue roll with lemon curd and cream
preFix.excerpt: Lemon meringue roulade is a flat sheet of soft meringue rolled around whipped cream and sharp lemon curd. The flavour takes the same notes as a lemon meringue pie and puts them into a roll that can be made a day ahead. The meringue is deliberately underbaked to stay pliable.
preFix.sourceNotes: Meringue roulades in British domestic baking grew from the tradition of Swiss rolls, adapted for home cooks who wanted a more impressive centrepiece without the technical demands of a layered gateau. The lemon curd filling is a natural pairing for meringue and appears widely in British baking books from the 1980s onward. The method of using icing sugar on the paper to prevent sticking dates from at least the same period.

content.text: The lemon curd can be shop-bought or made a few days ahead and refrigerated. The meringue is made exactly as for a pavlova: egg whites whisked to stiff peaks, then sugar added gradually until the mixture is thick and glossy. Unlike a pavlova, this is spread into a rectangle and baked for a shorter time so it stays soft enough to roll without shattering.

Heat the oven to 180 degrees C fan. Line a 33 x 23 cm tin with baking paper with an overhang. Whisk 4 egg whites to stiff peaks. Add 200 g caster sugar a tablespoon at a time, whisking between each addition, until thick, glossy, and the sugar has dissolved. Whisk in 1 tsp cornflour and 1 tsp white wine vinegar. Spread evenly into the tin. Bake for 18 to 22 minutes until the surface is set and pale; the inside should still give slightly when pressed.

Lay a sheet of baking paper on the work surface and dust with icing sugar. Turn the meringue out, peel off the lining paper, and leave to cool for 20 minutes. Fold the lemon curd through the whipped cream until marbled but not fully blended. Spread the cream mixture over the meringue, leaving a 2 cm border at one short edge. Roll from that edge, using the paper to guide. Place join-side down on a plate and refrigerate for 1 hour before serving.

items.problem: Meringue is too sticky to peel off the paper
items.solution: The meringue needs more time in the oven or the oven temperature was too low. It should feel set and dry on the surface. Bake for a further 3 to 5 minutes, or check the oven temperature with a thermometer.

items.problem: The cream mixture is too runny to hold its shape
items.solution: The cream was not whipped enough before the curd was added, or the curd was too warm. Whip cream to soft peaks that hold their shape, then fold in cold curd. Chill the filled roulade before serving.
